b:T4e852,<div class="specification">
<p>Three-toed sloths (<em>Bradypus variegatus</em>) are placental mammals that live in trees in Central and South America. They eat leaves and fruit and get almost all their water from succulent plants.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;">[Source: Adapted from Laube, S., 2003. Three-toed-sloth (<em>Bradypus variegatus</em>), Lake Gatun, Republic of Panama. [image online] Available at: https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/User:Bradipus#/media/File:Bradypus.jpg] </p>
</div><div class="specification">
<p>Three-toed sloths change their body posture in response to the temperature of their environment (ambient temperature). Researchers assessed posture on a scale from 1 to 6, with 1 being when the sloth was curled into a tight ball and 6 when it had all limbs spread. The percentage of time the sloths were observed in each position was recorded at ambient temperatures from 22 °C to 34 °C. The researchers also measured the body temperature of the sloths over the same range of ambient temperatures.</p>
<p><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;">[Source: Adapted from Cliffe, R.N., Scantlebury, D.M., Kennedy, S.J., Avey-Arroyo, J., Mindich, D. and Wilson, R.P., 2018. The metabolic response of the Bradypus sloth to temperature. <em>PeerJ</em>, [e-journal] 6: e5600. <a href="http://dx.doi.org/10.7717/peerj.5600">http://dx.doi.org/10.7717/peerj.5600</a>. Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License <a href="https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/">https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/</a>.]</p>
</div><div class="specification">
<p>The daily food intake of three-toed sloths and daily ambient temperatures were monitored over a 160-day period from February to early July. The graphs show the mean results.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;">[Source: Cliffe et al. (2015), Sloths like it hot: ambient temperature modulates food intake in the brown-throated sloth (<em>Bradypus variegatus</em>). PeerJ 3:e875; DOI <a href="10.7717/peerj.875">10.7717/peerj.875</a> Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License <a href="https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/">https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/</a>.]</p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>State the relationship between sloth body temperature and ambient temperature.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Explain how this relationship differs from that in humans.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Describe the trend in body posture as ambient temperature rises from 22 to 34 °C.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Suggest reasons for this trend.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>The mean daily food intake fluctuated from day to day. State the month that contains the day on which the mean intake of food was highest.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">f.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Outline the relationship between ambient daily temperature and food intake in March.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">g.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Suggest, with a reason, how the activity of the sloth varies with ambient temperature.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">h.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>State <strong>one</strong> feature of the sloth that would indicate it is a mammal.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">i.</div>
</div>c:T9c4,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>body temperature increases with ambient temperature / positive correlation;</p>
<p><em>Since direct can be either –/+, no credit for direct correlation alone.</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>humans maintain/regulate a constant body temperature at different ambient temperatures/maintained by homeostasis;</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>As ambient temperature increases, the sloth spends more time with limbs spread<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>as ambient temperature increases the sloth spends less time curled in a ball<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>as ambient temperature rises, the posture changes from 1 to 6;</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<ol style="list-style-type:lower-alpha;">
<li>less surface area is exposed when curled up<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>more surface area is exposed when all limbs spread;</li>
<li>curled position prevents heat loss/provides warmth<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>stretched out position allows more heat loss/body cooled;</li>
</ol>
<div class="question_part_label">b.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>May;</p>
<div class="question_part_label">f.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<ol style="list-style-type:lower-alpha;">
<li>food intake rises as daily temperature increases / positive correlation;</li>
<li>the lowest food intake corresponds to the lowest temperature;</li>
</ol>
<div class="question_part_label">g.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<ol style="list-style-type:lower-alpha;">
<li>the sloth will be more active at higher temperatures as it takes in more food for energy;</li>
<li>as temperature rises, the sloth uncurls to dissipate/lose heat;</li>
</ol>
<p><em>Reason required</em>.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">h.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<ol style="list-style-type:lower-alpha;">
<li>mammals have mammary glands;</li>
<li>produce milk for their offspring;</li>
<li>bodies covered in hair/fur;</li>
</ol>
<p><em>Characteristic must be exclusive to mammals.</em></p>
<p><em>If more than one answer, use the first one given.</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">i.</div>
</div>d:T727,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
[N/A]
<div class="question_part_label">a.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>Often steady or constant body temperatures for humans was given rather than homeostasis. Some candidates erroneously described humans as warming up in response to cold ambient temperatures.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
[N/A]
<div class="question_part_label">b.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>There were a few beautiful responses where surface area was specifically incorporated into the answer achieving maximum marks.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
[N/A]
<div class="question_part_label">f.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
[N/A]
<div class="question_part_label">g.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>Good reasoning was not seen often, best answers needed some of the elements in this linkage: higher temperature led to uncurling which led to movement which led to eating because of energy needs; "sloth prefers staying curled up so cold does not affect them" or "sloth enjoys staying curled up" were not acceptable</p>
<div class="question_part_label">h.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>Have hair/fur or feed young with milk were the best answers. Many students wrote "give birth to live young;" While this is true of most mammals, monotremes lay eggs. Furthermore, some other animals give birth to live young (ovoviviparous) such as some reptiles and some groups of fish. We credited only characteristics exclusive to all mammals.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">i.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/4/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","4"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"22M.2.SL.TZ1.1"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"SL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","2"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"22M"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","6-6-hormones-homeostasis-and-reproduction, 4-2-energy-flow, 5-3-classification-of-biodiversity"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$c"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$d"}}]]}]]}]
